surface.name: Get started with your Developer Sandbox learning path
surface.selection_basis: The OpenShift product page's primary action is Try it in the Developer Sandbox, the Getting Started page links the free Developer Sandbox for hands-on experience, and the Sandbox page recommends Get started with your Developer Sandbox. That official beginner learning path has three ordered resources and an explicit terminal resource.

alternatives_considered.name: OpenShift Local
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This requires a local OpenShift installation, while the product's primary try action points to the no-setup browser Sandbox.

alternatives_considered.name: OpenShift Container Platform installation
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is an operator and infrastructure installation route rather than the vendor-presented no-cost developer entry.

alternatives_considered.name: Access Developer Sandbox only
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Entering the Sandbox is a prerequisite and access milestone; the recommended learning path continues through two application builds.

entry_point.developer_intent: Try Red Hat OpenShift through the vendor-presented free Developer Sandbox and complete its recommended getting-started learning path.
assumed_prior_state: Discovery is complete and the developer has selected OpenShift.
The developer has not yet provisioned a Developer Sandbox cluster.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Complete Get started with your Developer Sandbox by building an application from source code and then building an application from a container image.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The developer has entered a free OpenShift Developer Sandbox, deployed and opened a source-built application, and created a second deployment from the specified container image.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: For the source build, the application icon ring changes from light blue to dark blue and the external link opens the app in a browser. In the terminal learning-path resource, selecting Create deploys quay.io/rhdevelopers/intro-front from a container image, after which the page ends with invitations to experiment and learn more.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The official learning path lists three ordered resources. Its second resource explicitly congratulates the developer for building from source and sends them to the third resource. The third and terminal resource has the developer create a deployment from a container image, then ends the path with Play around, Try things, Break things, and Learn more.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the terminal state of the recommended three-resource Developer Sandbox learning path. The record stops before optional experimentation, additional products, CLI access, and production OpenShift installation.

prerequisites.requirement: A Red Hat account is required to start the Developer Sandbox.
A valid phone number must be validated to access the Developer Sandbox.
A free OpenShift cluster from Developer Sandbox.
A web browser; Red Hat states no local installation is required for the Sandbox path.

primary_path.action: Open OpenShift Getting Started and follow the free Developer Sandbox path.
details: The product page presents Try it in the Developer Sandbox and the Getting Started page recommends hands-on Sandbox experience.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No wait is documented at this documentation step.

primary_path.action: Select Start your sandbox for free and sign in with a Red Hat account.
details: If no account exists, select Register for a Red Hat account, complete registration, and return.
primary_path.success_signal: The Start using your sandbox action is shown.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The official access article does not enumerate the account-registration fields.

primary_path.action: Complete the Sandbox phone-number validation required by Red Hat.
details: Red Hat says the valid phone number is used to reduce fraudulent account creation.
primary_path.success_signal: The Sandbox access flow can continue.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The FAQ does not document the exact verification channel or wait.

primary_path.action: Select Start using your sandbox, then select DevSandbox on the OpenShift Dedicated login page.
Select the Red Hat OpenShift tile and choose Launch.
primary_path.success_signal: A Welcome to the Developer Perspective message appears.

primary_path.action: Open the recommended Get started with your Developer Sandbox learning path.
details: The path contains Overview, Build your application from source code, and Build your application from a container image.
primary_path.success_signal: The Overview and ordered resources are visible.

primary_path.action: In OpenShift Topology, open Add to Project and select Import from Git.
primary_path.success_signal: The form asks for a Git repository URL.

primary_path.action: Enter one of the official sample repository URLs.
details: The lesson provides C#, Go, Node.js, PHP, Python, and Ruby samples.
OpenShift copies and inspects the repository and chooses a build strategy; the lesson focuses on a Builder image and source-to-image.
primary_path.success_signal: OpenShift presents deployment configuration for the imported source.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Repository inspection can choose Builder image, Dockerfile, or DevFile; the lesson requires the Builder image route.

primary_path.action: Select Deployment as Resource type and select Create.
primary_path.success_signal: The application icon appears in Topology.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The lesson states the build takes a few minutes.

primary_path.action: Complete the source deployment and change the application icon ring from light blue to dark blue.
details: Dark blue means the application is ready to go.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A ring that does not become dark blue has not reached the lesson's ready signal.

primary_path.action: Select the external-link icon and open the source-built application in a browser.
primary_path.success_signal: The application opens in the browser; the lesson says the application has been built from source code.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An inaccessible route fails the explicit test step.

primary_path.action: Continue to the terminal resource, return to Topology, open Add to Project, and select Container image.
primary_path.success_signal: The form requests a fully qualified image name.

primary_path.action: Enter quay.io/rhdevelopers/intro-front as the fully qualified container image.
primary_path.success_signal: The form accepts the image name.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No alternative image is used in the official terminal resource.

primary_path.action: Select Deployment as Resource type and select Create.
details: This is the final instructed action in the terminal learning-path resource.
primary_path.output: OpenShift deployment from the specified container image
primary_path.success_signal: The deployment is created and the terminal resource moves to optional play, break, and learn-more suggestions.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The resource does not document a separate container-image readiness wait or browser verification after Create.

branches.condition: The developer lacks a Red Hat account.
branches.path: Select Register for a Red Hat account, complete the registration process, and return to Sandbox sign-in.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an account-creation gate whose fields are not enumerated in the inspected OpenShift access article.

branches.condition: The developer selects a different official sample language.
branches.path: Use the provided C#, Go, Node.js, PHP, Python, or Ruby sample repository; OpenShift inspects the repository and selects the appropriate Builder image.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the sample code and Builder image but retains the same source-build readiness and browser signals.

branches.condition: Repository inspection selects Dockerfile or DevFile rather than Builder image and source-to-image.
branches.path: The learning resource lists these as possible OpenShift strategies but explicitly focuses its first source-build lesson on the Builder image route.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Leaves the canonical learning-path path and requires different build details.

friction_gates.description: A Red Hat account is required; new users must register and return.
A valid phone number must be validated for Developer Sandbox access.
The learning path requires a free OpenShift Developer Sandbox cluster.
The developer chooses one of six official sample-language repositories for the source build.
The source build and deployment takes a few minutes before the icon ring becomes dark blue.
The terminal resource requires the fully qualified quay.io/rhdevelopers/intro-front image name and Deployment resource type.

time_to_first_success.value: 20 minutes in the learning-path header; 20-30 minutes in the overview's duration section

sources.title: Getting started with Red Hat OpenShift
evidence_supported: Developer Sandbox as vendor-presented hands-on route

sources.title: Get started with your Developer Sandbox
sections_used: How long will this learning path take

sources.title: How to access the Developer Sandbox for Red Hat OpenShift
sections_used: Access the Developer Sandbox for Red Hat OpenShift

sources.title: Build your application from source code
sections_used: Build the application from source code

sources.title: Build your application from a container image
sections_used: Build the application from a container image
evidence_supported: Required image, resource type, and Create action

uncertainties.question: Which account-registration fields and phone-verification mechanism appear in the current signup UI?
uncertainties.impact: The exact number of identity and verification steps before Sandbox access cannot be counted.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official OpenShift access article says to complete registration and return, and the FAQ states phone validation is required, but neither enumerates the current registration fields or verification channel.

uncertainties.question: Is the intended learning-path duration 20 minutes or 20-30 minutes?
uncertainties.impact: The vendor time claim is internally inconsistent.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The same official path displays 20 mins in its header and 20-30 minutes in the overview body.

excluded_after_success: Optional Sandbox experimentation after the terminal resource
Additional OpenShift tutorials, Dev Spaces, Pipelines, GitOps, AI, or service-mesh paths
